title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new digital detox retreat they attended.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I’m still feeling remarkably calm after that weekend without my phone. || Woman: [1]I found the mandatory silence during meals a bit forced[/1], to be honest. It felt a bit performative rather than actually relaxing. || Man: I see what you mean. It was a bit intense. || Woman: [2]What I really appreciated, though, was the guided nature walk[/2]. It was so nice to actually look at the trees instead of taking photos of them. || Man: I agree. It made me realize how much of life I view through a lens. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a great way to reset your habits, even if some of the rules were a bit over-the-top.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’m still feeling remarkably calm after that weekend without my phone.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the mandatory silence during meals a bit forced, to be honest. It felt a bit performative rather than actually relaxing.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. It was a bit intense.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What I really appreciated, though, was the guided nature walk. It was so nice to actually look at the trees instead of taking photos of them.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It made me realize how much of life I view through a lens.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a great way to reset your habits, even if some of the rules were a bit over-the-top.

questions.question: What did the woman dislike about the retreat?

options.C: the poor quality of the facilities

questions.explanation: She describes the mandatory silence as 'forced' and 'performative' [1].

questions.question: Both speakers agree that the retreat helped them to
questions.explanation: They both highlight the value of looking at nature directly rather than through a phone lens [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a new project management tool.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, how are you finding 'Task-Master'? I think the Gantt charts are quite helpful for visualizing the timeline. || Woman: [3]I suspect the interface was designed by someone who’s never actually managed a complex project[/3]. It’s far too cluttered. || Man: It does take a while to find anything. But the automated reminders are a lifesaver. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that it’s actually taking me longer to update my tasks than it is to actually do them[/4], which is quite frustrating. || Man: I agree. It’s a bit of an administrative burden. || Woman: Exactly. If a tool doesn't save you time, then what’s the point of using it in the first place?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, how are you finding 'Task-Master'? I think the Gantt charts are quite helpful for visualizing the timeline.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the interface was designed by someone who’s never actually managed a complex project. It’s far too cluttered.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It does take a while to find anything. But the automated reminders are a lifesaver.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that it’s actually taking me longer to update my tasks than it is to actually do them, which is quite frustrating.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s a bit of an administrative burden.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. If a tool doesn't save you time, then what’s the point of using it in the first place?

questions.question: The woman believes the software is

options.A: too basic for her team's requirements.
options.B: poorly organized from a user perspective.
options.C: too expensive for the features it offers.

questions.explanation: She thinks it's 'far too cluttered' and designed by someone 'who's never managed a project' [3].

questions.question: What is the speakers' main complaint about the tool?

options.A: the lack of integration with other apps
options.B: the excessive amount of time it requires
options.C: the inaccuracy of the progress reports

questions.explanation: They agree it's an 'administrative burden' that takes longer to update than the work itself [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a recently opened community garden.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I went down to the 'Green-Patch' yesterday. It’s amazing what they’ve done with that old abandoned lot. || Woman: [5]I found the scheduling for the communal tools to be a bit unorganized[/5]. There was no trowel left when I arrived. || Man: I agree, it’s a bit of a free-for-all at the moment. || Woman: [6]I did appreciate the sense of community spirit, though[/6]. I’ve met more of my neighbours in the last week than in the last three years. || Man: I agree. It’s a very welcoming space. || Woman: Definitely. I’ll just bring my own equipment next time to avoid the frustration at the shed.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I went down to the 'Green-Patch' yesterday. It’s amazing what they’ve done with that old abandoned lot.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the scheduling for the communal tools to be a bit unorganized. There was no trowel left when I arrived.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree, it’s a bit of a free-for-all at the moment.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did appreciate the sense of community spirit, though. I’ve met more of my neighbours in the last week than in the last three years.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s a very welcoming space.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. I’ll just bring my own equipment next time to avoid the frustration at the shed.

questions.question: What did the woman find annoying about her visit?

options.A: the poor condition of the plants
options.C: the noisy environment in the park

questions.explanation: She notes that 'there was no trowel left' and the tool shed was 'unorganized' [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the garden's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'sense of community spirit' and the 'welcoming space' [6].
